AI Product Management — Applied Workflow Tooling

Designs shouldn't need to be retyped into tickets.

A working pipeline from Figma to Jira, via Claude. Two passes on the same screen — one transcribed it, one thought it through.

Figma MCP Jira MCP Claude Code GitHub →
→
Before: a PM retypes it. After: it writes itself.

PMs manually rewrite every finished design into a Jira ticket, screen by screen. This automates that step.

How it works

Claude reads the Figma file through Figma's MCP server, and creates the ticket through Atlassian's — no copy-paste.

The live MCP call, in Claude Code
The iteration

Same screen. One instruction changed.

First pass

Every field, every color, faithfully transcribed. Accurate — and read like a spec.

Second pass — one instruction changed

Write it like a PM. It came back with a user story, a real priority reason, and 7 open questions a spec would never ask — like what happens when someone disables a model that's live.

The point

A transcription can't ask those questions. Directed well, the same tool can.

The receipts
First pass — full ticket
Second pass — full ticket
v1 — translates structure, and when directed, intent. Doesn't yet flag edge cases on its own. That's next. GitHub →
